videoTitle: How to build a 10-cent AI brain
notes: Four cards (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Grok) each with a small 'Memory' label and padlock icon, arranged in an irregular constellation. Yellow X marks sit between adjacent cards to mean 'cannot share memory'. Cards reveal one at a time. Title 'The Memory Silo Problem' in muted gold sits centered. There's a duplicate title at the top edge — looks like a thin top-banner echo. Karaoke caption pinned to bottom. Outro of the short.

videoTitle: RTX 5090, Mac Studio, or DGX Spark? I tried all three.
notes: Section header 'Cloud Or Local' (large white sans serif) with subtitle 'not a binary' (muted gray). Dark slate background with a soft radial vignette/glow. Large translucent helmet+glasses watermark in lower-left corner of the frame area. Fades in, holds ~6-8s, fades out.

videoTitle: RTX 5090, Mac Studio, or DGX Spark? I tried all three.
notes: Sequence: (a) prior 'Runtime Layer / local becomes usable' title card fades out, (b) cards appear one-at-a-time horizontally: 'MODEL / Weights / raw model' (gray accent), 'SERVE / Runtime / loads model' (blue accent), 'API / Endpoint / shared shape' (green accent), 'USE / Tools / talk to it' (orange accent), (c) right-pointing chevron-arrows draw between each card in matching colors, (d) caption pill 'Runtime makes local AI feel normal' fades in below — 'Runtime' highlighted orange. The whole sequence is the pedagogical payoff of the section.

videoTitle: RTX 5090, Mac Studio, or DGX Spark? I tried all three.
notes: Title card 'Runtime Defaults' with subtitle 'simple starting path'. Identical visual treatment to 'Cloud Or Local'. The end of the window catches the card during its fade-out (text noticeably dimmed).

videoTitle: RTX 5090, Mac Studio, or DGX Spark? I tried all three.
notes: Two-column 'Default vs Better' contrast: LEFT column has a short orange/red horizontal underline + uppercase tracked label 'DEFAULT' + headline 'Cloud Home' + muted orange subtitle 'rented memory'. RIGHT column has a blue underline + 'BETTER' label + 'Cloud Guest' + blue subtitle 'specialist'. Centered above the two columns is the word 'TO' in gray. Looks like both columns slide in from their respective sides; 'TO' fades in last as the bridge.

videoTitle: GPT-5.5 vs Claude vs Gemini: The Real Difference Nobody's Talking About
notes: Title card 'Moving Frontier' / 'ambition follows'. Dark slate slab, white sans serif headline, muted gray subtitle, helmet watermark lower-left, soft radial glow. SAME template as iUSdS-6uwr4 cards — confirms reuse.

videoTitle: GPT-5.5 vs Claude vs Gemini: The Real Difference Nobody's Talking About
notes: Title card 'Reference Workflow' / 'taste into build'. Identical visual treatment. Two instances of this template in one video plus the iUSdS-6uwr4 video = at least 4 confirmed instances across the corpus = clearly a reusable section divider.

videoTitle: The Infrastructure Nightmare Nobody Is Talking About
notes: Two-presenter podcast frame: exact 50/50 vertical split, Nate on left (his usual book-lined room), guest (Asian woman in purple hoodie, different room with shelves of decor) on right. Each half retains its own lighting and ambient room. Persistent throughout the entire 46-minute video — not an episodic animation but the structural visual mode. Worth documenting because we don't currently have a podcast/interview composition.

videoTitle: These 5 Infrastructure Giants Secretly Rule AI
notes: Negative finding: despite the 'listicle' framing in the title, the entire video is talking head with no item-reveal motion graphics. The only persistent overlay is a lower-right 'read more on substack' chip. Documented here as a calibration point: Nate frequently does NOT animate listicles — counter-evidence to anyone expecting a NumberedListReveal.
